amber.description: Catalysts, regulatory proteins, molecular machines, receptors

black.description: Terminal products, cellular outcomes, equilibrium states, system endpoints

darkSkyBlue.description: Biochemical reactions, transport, modifications, transcription, translation

green.description: External signals, nutrient availability, environmental conditions, initial states

lightCyan.description: Metabolites, molecular complexes, cellular states, compounds

purple.description: Multi-component requirements, signal integration, convergence points

yellow.description: Decision points with alternative pathways (yes/no branching)

description: The secretory pathway in S. cerevisiae transports proteins from ER through Golgi to plasma membrane via COPII-coated vesicles. Each vesicle budding and fusion event requires: cargo selection, coat assembly (Sar1, Sec23/24, Sec13/31 for COPII), vesicle scission, coat disassembly, tethering, SNARE pairing, and membrane fusion. Rab GTPases (Ypt1, Sec4) regulate vesicle identity and targeting. The pathway has extremely high AND gate requirements - all components must be present and properly assembled for successful transport. Nobel Prize to Schekman, Rothman, Südhof.

Heterodimer?} O -->|No| P[Coat Assembly Fails] O -->|Yes| Q[Sec23/24 Binds Sar1-GTP] Q --> R[Cargo Binding by Sec24] R --> S{AND: Cargo

Heterotetramer?} U -->|No| P U -->|Yes| V[Sec13/31 Polymerization] V --> W[Outer Coat Cage Formation] W --> X[Cuboctahedral Lattice] X --> Y[Complete COPII Coat] %% Vesicle Scission Y --> Z{AND: Membrane
Fission Factors?} Z -->|No| AA[Budding Stalled] Z -->|Yes| AB[Membrane Scission] AB --> AC[Vesicle Released from ER] AC --> AD[Free COPII Vesicle] %% Coat Disassembly AD --> AE[Sec23 GAP Activity] AE --> AF[Sar1-GTP Hydrolysis] AF --> AG[Sar1-GDP + Pi] AG --> AH[Sar1 Membrane Dissociation] AH --> AI[Coat Disassembly] AI --> AJ[Naked Vesicle] %% Ypt1 Rab GTPase Recruitment AJ --> AK{AND: Ypt1-GTP
Present?} AK -->|No| AL[/Vesicle Cannot Dock/] AK -->|Yes| AM[Ypt1 Marks Vesicle Identity] %% Tethering to Golgi AM --> AN[TRAPP Complex Recognition] AN --> AO{AND: All TRAPP
Subunits?} AO -->|No| AL AO -->|Yes| AP[TRAPP I Tethering Complex] AP --> AQ[Long-Range Vesicle Capture] AQ --> AR[Vesicle Tethered to Golgi] %% SNARE Pairing AR --> AS[v-SNARE Bet1/Bos1] AS --> AT[t-SNAREs Sed5/Sec22] AT --> AU{AND: All 4

Zippering?} AZ -->|No| AV AZ -->|Yes| BA[SNARE Zippering] BA --> BB[Membranes Pulled Together] BB --> BC[Hemifusion Intermediate] %% Membrane Fusion BC --> BD{AND: Sec17/18
Present?} BD -->|No| BE[Fusion Incomplete] BD -->|Yes| BF[Full Membrane Fusion] BF --> BG[Pore Formation] BG --> BH[Cargo Released into Golgi] BH --> BI[Cis-SNARE Complex] %% SNARE Recycling BI --> BJ[Sec18/NSF ATPase] BJ --> BK[Sec17/α-SNAP Adaptor] BK --> BL{AND: ATP
Hydrolysis?} BL -->|No| BM[SNAREs Stuck] BL -->|Yes| BN[SNARE Complex Disassembly] BN --> BO[SNAREs Recycled] BO --> BP[Ready for Next Round] %% Golgi Processing BH --> BQ{OR: Next
Destination?} BQ -->|Plasma Membrane| BR[Sec4-Mediated Secretion] BQ -->|Vacuole| BS[VPS Pathway] BQ -->|ER Return| BT[COPI Retrograde] BR --> BU[Exocyst Complex] BU --> BV{AND: All 8

name: Vesicle Trafficking and COPII-Mediated ER-to-Golgi Transport
notes: Vesicle trafficking has VERY high AND gates (13) - every component required for assembly and fusion. Nobel Prize work.
scientificAccuracy: Based on Nobel Prize-winning research on vesicle trafficking. All components validated in yeast genetic screens.

sources.title: COPII coat formation, vesicle budding, and cargo selection
sources.authors: Aridor M, Barlowe C, Schekman R.

sources.title: A budding role for Sar1p: the role of a small GTP-binding protein in ER export
Organization of the ER-Golgi interface for protein transport
